pass1_value.note: The 'Create an API Key' tutorial shows how a user in the Atlas UI can create API keys without human approval. Keys are created by project owners/organization admins; signup and free tier are available.

final_value.note: MongoDB Atlas docs describe creating (programmatic) API keys directly in the Atlas UI via Project Settings → Access Manager → API Keys → Create API Key, requiring Organization Owner or Project Owner role—indicating self-serve credential creation for eligible users.

pass1_value.note: Public REST API docs plus self-serve API key and OAuth2 service-account flows exist; credentials can be obtained via the Atlas UI or service account token flow.

final_value.note: Public REST API docs exist, and Atlas supports self-serve creation of programmatic API keys in the Atlas UI for users with the required roles (Org Owner/Project Owner), enabling immediate authenticated API access.
